outbound_official.notes: Ireland's Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) publishes a general outbound travel page at ireland.ie/en/dfa/overseas-travel/visas-for-irish-people-going-abroad/ and per-country travel advice at ireland.ie/en/dfa/overseas-travel/advice/ (legacy: dfa.ie/travel/travel-advice/a-z-list-of-countries/). Neither page publishes a consolidated machine-readable or tabular list of visa-free / visa-on-arrival / e-visa / ETA destinations for Irish ordinary-passport holders - they only give generic guidance ('contact the nearest Embassy or Consulate of the country you plan to visit'). Entry requirements for each destination are embedded within individual country travel-advice pages and are NOT aggregated into a single outbound visa-matrix page analogous to India's MEA VFFIN. Consequently no official single-source outbound list exists; rely on destination-side data and individual DFA country advice pages.

visa_free.nationality: EU/EEA member state nationals (Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland)
visa_free.notes: EU/EEA and Swiss nationals enjoy freedom of movement rights under EU Treaty Rights; no visa required. Ireland is not part of Schengen but is EU member.

visa_policy.notes: Ireland is NOT part of the Schengen Area; it sets its own visa policy. A Schengen visa does not entitle entry to Ireland. The official PDF list was last updated 12 May 2025; ongoing amendments via statutory instrument. Max stay for non-visa required nationals is 90 days at the discretion of the immigration officer. Ireland does not operate an ETA or ETIAS system - the UK ETA only applies to Northern Ireland. The Short Stay Visa Waiver Programme (SSVWP) allows certain visa-required nationals who hold a valid UK short-stay visa to enter Ireland without an Irish visa for up to 90 days; covered countries as of May 2025: Bosnia and Herzegovina, Kosovo, Montenegro, Republic of North Macedonia, Serbia, Türkiye (Eastern Europe); Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia (Middle East); India, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, People's Republic of China, Philippines, Thailand, Uzbekistan, Vietnam (Asia); Colombia, Peru (South America); and Ukraine (also unconditionally visa-free). As of 1 June 2026, visa applicants may no longer appeal the refusal of a short-stay visa. Recent removals from visa-free list: Eswatini and Lesotho removed 10 March 2025 (S.I. 68/2025); Trinidad and Tobago removed 12 May 2025 (S.I. 164/2025); Nicaragua, Saint Kitts and Nevis, and Saint Lucia removed effective 15 June 2026 (S.I. 242/2026, all passport types including diplomatic). Georgia removed from Schedule 2 (diplomatic passport exemption) effective 15 June 2026 (S.I. 242/2026). European Agreement on the Abolition of Visas for Refugees temporarily suspended by Ireland from 19 July (for 12 months) - holders of 1951 travel documents now require an Irish entry visa; holders of 1954 Convention Travel Documents issued by Ireland remain exempt. S.I. 17/2026 and S.I. 242/2026 are the only two visa statutory instruments enacted in 2026 (confirmed via irishstatutebook.ie as of 2026-06-27).

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Visit/Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals wishing to visit Ireland for tourism, sightseeing, or general visiting purposes for up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Single entry fee is approximately €60 (~$65 USD); multiple entry fee is approximately €100 (~$108 USD). Applied for online via AVATS system. Valid for countries whose nationals require a visa per the official ISD list. Processing times vary by location and period. A multiple-entry C visa may be granted to frequent travellers.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Visit Family/Friend Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals visiting family members or friends residing lawfully in Ireland for up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the short-stay C visit visa. Applicant must demonstrate genuine family or friendship ties. Host in Ireland must provide an invitation letter and proof of lawful residence.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals attending business meetings, conferences, or commercial activities in Ireland for up to 90 days without taking up employment.
visa_types.notes: Covers short-term business activities only - no remuneration from an Irish source permitted. Separate from the employment visa. Conference/event visas and training visas are related sub-categories.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Conference/Event Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals attending a specific conference, seminar, exhibition, or event in Ireland for less than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Must provide conference/event invitation, registration, and itinerary. No remuneration from an Irish source permitted.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Training Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals coming to Ireland for a short training programme (under 90 days) with an Irish employer or organisation.
visa_types.notes: Training must be short-term and not constitute employment in Ireland. Training letter from the host organisation required.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Performance/Tournament Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals coming to Ireland to participate in a sporting event, musical performance, or similar tournament for less than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Covers sportspeople and performers visiting for a specific event. Evidence of the event or tournament invitation required.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Join Ship Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required non-EEA seafarers joining a ship docked in an Irish port for less than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Applicant must have evidence of employment on the vessel and the vessel's docking details.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Exam Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals coming to Ireland solely to sit a specific professional or academic examination.
visa_types.notes: Applicant must provide confirmation of examination registration, date, and location in Ireland.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Medical Treatment Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals seeking medical treatment at an Irish hospital or medical facility for a period of up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Must provide a letter from an Irish medical professional confirming treatment, estimated duration, and costs. Proof of sufficient funds to cover treatment and repatriation required.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Marriage Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals coming to Ireland to marry or enter a civil partnership, for a stay of up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Applicant must provide evidence of the planned marriage/civil partnership in Ireland including civil notification to the Registrar. Note: UK BIVS visa does NOT cover visitor in transit or visitor for marriage/civil partnership.

visa_types.name: Short Stay 'C' Study Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals enrolling in a short course of study in Ireland lasting less than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: For short courses only (under 90 days). Applicants must provide a letter of acceptance from a recognised Irish educational institution.

visa_types.name: Long Stay 'D' Study Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals intending to study full-time at a recognised Irish educational institution for more than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Long-stay D visa fee is approximately €100 (~$108 USD). The D visa is a national long-stay visa valid for the duration of studies. After arrival, student must register with Immigration Service Delivery to obtain an Irish Residence Permit (Stamp 2). Must be enrolled in an approved full-time course. English language requirements and financial sufficiency proofs required.

visa_types.name: Long Stay 'D' Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals who have been granted an Irish employment permit and need entry authorisation to take up employment in Ireland.
visa_types.notes: Applicant must first obtain an employment permit from the Department of Enterprise, Trade and Employment (enterprise.gov.ie). The D employment visa is required only by visa-required nationals; non-visa-required nationals with an employment permit can enter directly. After entry, holder must register for an Irish Residence Permit (IRP).

visa_types.name: Long Stay 'D' Join Family Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals seeking to join a family member who is an Irish citizen or has long-term immigration permission in Ireland.
visa_types.notes: Covers joining an Irish national or a non-EEA national with long-term permission. Applicant must demonstrate genuine family relationship. Processing can take several months. After arrival must register for an IRP (Stamp 3 or Stamp 4 depending on circumstances). Joining EU/EEA/Swiss nationals is handled under EU Treaty Rights pathway.

visa_types.name: Critical Skills Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: For highly skilled non-EEA workers in occupations on the Critical Skills Occupations List or earning above €68,911 annually, allowing them to work in Ireland for up to 2 years initially.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €1,000 (~$1,080 USD); 90% refunded if unsuccessful. No Labour Market Needs Test required. Minimum annual salary €40,904 (or €36,848 for recent graduates), or any occupation at €68,911+. Job offer must be for minimum 2 years. After permit expires, holder may apply directly for Stamp 4 (permission to work without permit). Pathway to citizenship after 5 years residence. Governed by Employment Permits Act 2024.

visa_types.name: General Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: For non-EEA workers in occupations not on the excluded list, subject to a Labour Market Needs Test showing no suitable EEA workers were available.
visa_types.notes: Fee: €500 for ≤6 months, €1,000 for 6–24 months; renewal €750/€1,500. 90% refund if unsuccessful. Minimum salary €36,605/year (lower for healthcare roles at €32,691). Labour Market Needs Test required for most occupations (EURES ad for at least 28 days). Initial permit up to 24 months; renewable for further 3 years. 50:50 rule requires 50% of employer workforce to be EEA nationals (exceptions for startups).

visa_types.name: Intra-Company Transfer Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: For senior management, key personnel, or trainees transferring from a foreign branch to an Irish branch of a multinational company.
visa_types.notes: Fee: €500 (≤6 months) or €1,000 (6–24 months); renewal €500–€1,500. Senior/key personnel minimum salary €49,523; trainees minimum €36,605. Prior employment with foreign employer: 6 months for senior/key, 1 month for trainees. Maximum 5 years total stay. Does not accrue rights toward long-term residency. No Labour Market Needs Test required.

visa_types.name: Dependant/Partner/Spouse Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows family members (dependants, partners, spouses) of Critical Skills Employment Permit holders or researchers to work in Ireland.
visa_types.notes: No fee for this permit. Applicant must already reside continuously in Ireland and have been determined a dependant by the Department of Justice. Cannot be used to apply from outside Ireland. Employment as a domestic operative is not permitted. Tied to primary permit holder's permission duration. Minimum wage applies.

visa_types.name: Contract for Services Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: For non-EEA employees of foreign contractors being transferred to Ireland to fulfil a specific service contract with an Irish business.
visa_types.notes: Fee: €500 (≤6 months) or €1,000 (6–24 months); renewal €750–€1,500. The foreign contractor must have a valid service contract with an Irish-registered company. Employee remains employed by and paid by the foreign contractor. No Labour Market Needs Test required.

visa_types.name: Reactivation Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EEA nationals who previously held a valid employment permit but fell out of the employment permit system through no fault of their own to re-enter the legal work system.
visa_types.notes: Fee: €500 (≤6 months) or €1,000 (6–24 months); renewal €750–€1,500. Designed to address situations where workers became undocumented due to employer failures or exploitation. Must demonstrate previous valid status and involuntary loss of status.

visa_types.name: Internship Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: For full-time international students studying outside Ireland who wish to complete a work placement/internship in a critical skills occupation with an Irish employer.
visa_types.notes: Fee: €500 (≤6 months) or €1,000 (up to 12 months). 90% refunded if unsuccessful. Maximum duration 12 months, non-renewable. Must be enrolled full-time at a third-level institution outside Ireland pursuing a degree or higher. Role must align with Critical Skills Occupations List. No Labour Market Needs Test. Does not accrue rights toward long-term residency.

visa_types.name: Sport and Cultural Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: For non-EEA nationals working in the sports or entertainment sectors in Ireland, including athletes, coaches, performers, and related roles.
visa_types.notes: Fee: €500 (≤6 months) or €1,000 (up to 24 months); renewal €750–€1,500. Full-time employment maximum 2 years initially, extendable up to 3 additional years. Seasonal employment also available. No Labour Market Needs Test required. Holder must remain with initial employer for 12 months (except in cases of redundancy).

visa_types.name: Exchange Agreement Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: For non-EEA nationals working in Ireland under an international bilateral reciprocal exchange agreement between Ireland and another country.
visa_types.notes: No fee for this permit. Covers non-EEA nationals participating in formal government-to-government exchange schemes. Duration and conditions depend on the specific bilateral agreement. No Labour Market Needs Test required.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Authorisation
visa_types.purpose: For young nationals of countries with a bilateral working holiday agreement with Ireland to live and work in Ireland for up to 12 months.
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of countries with bilateral working holiday agreements with Ireland (includes Australia, Canada, New Zealand, Argentina, Hong Kong, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, USA, and others). Typically for ages 18–35. Holder can take casual or temporary employment. No employment permit required under this scheme. Must be applied for from home country. Eligible nationalities and exact terms vary by bilateral agreement.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visa-required nationals transiting through Irish airports or seaports en route to another country.
visa_types.notes: Ireland is not part of the Schengen Area so Schengen transit rules do not apply. Transit visa may be required by nationals of certain countries even for airside (sterile transit) transit. Specific transit visa requirements depend on nationality and whether the transit involves passing through Irish immigration control (landside) or remaining airside. Applicants should check the ISD transit page for their specific nationality.

visa_types.name: Start-up Entrepreneur Programme (STEP)
visa_types.purpose: For non-EEA entrepreneurs with an innovative business proposal who wish to establish and develop a start-up business in Ireland.
visa_types.notes: Requires minimum €50,000 in funding (from personal resources, loan, business angel, VC, or Irish state agency grant). Business must not be retail, catering, or personal services. Must have potential to create 10 jobs and €1 million in sales within 3–4 years. Additional €30,000 per additional co-founder. Applications evaluated quarterly. Successful applicants receive 2-year residence permission. Visa-required nationals also receive a multiple-entry visa. Established 2012; guidelines updated September 2023.

credential.subtype: UK short-stay ('C') visitor visa (all standard visitor categories except visitor in transit and visitor for marriage or civil partnership)

conditional_access.conditions: Short-Stay Visa Waiver Programme (SSVWP). Must hold a valid UK short-stay ('C') visa (not an ETA, not a long-stay 'D' visa, not a BRP card). Traveller must have already landed and gained lawful entry into the UK on foot of the current UK visa before travelling to Ireland. Each distinct period of leave to remain in the UK (up to max 180 days) requires a prior legal entry into the UK. Maximum stay in Ireland is 90 days or the remaining time on UK permission, whichever is less. Note: for Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar and Peru nationals, a UK ETA cannot be used - only a pre-1 February 2024 short-stay UK visa qualifies. Ukraine nationals are covered but are also now unconditionally visa-free as of 25 February 2022. Belarus and Russia were removed from the SSVWP. Ecuador was never confirmed on the current official SSVWP list. Programme not reciprocal. SSVWP page last updated 9 May 2025.

credential.subtype: UK standard visitor visa endorsed 'BIVS' (all standard visitor categories max 6 months except visitor in transit and visitor for marriage/civil partnership; also Permitted Paid Engagement visas)

conditional_access.conditions: British-Irish Visa Scheme (BIVS). Visa must be endorsed with the letters 'BIVS' by UK visa officer (discretionary; no appeal against non-endorsement). Applicants must be resident in China (including HK/Macau) or India at time of application. Must first travel to the country that issued the visa (i.e., if UK-issued BIVS visa, must enter UK first). Single entry BIVS = single entry to Common Travel Area (CTA). Multiple entry: must re-enter issuing country first on each new entry to CTA. Duration up to 90 days or remaining UK permission, whichever shorter, as decided by Irish immigration officer on arrival. Ireland must be primary destination if applying for Irish BIVS visa. Note: HK/Macau passport holders are already visa-free for Ireland and do not need BIVS.

credential.subtype: Irish short-stay visa endorsed 'BIVS' (visit family/friend, visit tourist, conference/event, business)

conditional_access.conditions: British-Irish Visa Scheme (BIVS) - Irish visa variant. Visa must be endorsed with the letters 'BIVS'. Must first travel to Ireland (issuing country) before visiting UK. Allows travel within the Common Travel Area (Ireland and UK, excluding Isle of Man and Channel Islands). Duration in UK decided by UK immigration officer. Up to 90 days total. No UK transit visa required if transiting UK to reach Ireland provided travel into Ireland by 23:59 on the day after UK arrival.

credential.subtype: valid EU permanent residence card (as defined in EC Regulations 2006 / Directive 2004/38/EC)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid EU permanent residence card (issued under Directive 2004/38/EC) are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Article 3(b)(ii) of S.I. No. 473/2014 (as amended). Applies regardless of nationality.

credential.subtype: valid EU residence card - 'Residence card of a family member of a Union citizen' (Article 10, Directive 2004/38/EC)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid EU residence card issued under Article 10 of Directive 2004/38/EC (family members of Union citizens) are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Article 3(b)(iii) and Article 3(c) of S.I. No. 473/2014 (as amended). Applies regardless of nationality.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Albania are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of the Immigration Act 2004 (Visas) Order 2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Bosnia and Herzegovina are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Colombia are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days. Note: ordinary passport holders are also visa-free via the SSVWP (with valid UK visa).

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Indonesia are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Kosovo are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Kuwait are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Republic of Moldova are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Montenegro are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Republic of North Macedonia are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by the People's Republic of China are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 (as originally enacted, confirmed in S.I. 17/2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Peru are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Qatar are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Serbia are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by South Africa are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as inserted by S.I. No. 335/2024 (in force 10 July 2024) and confirmed in S.I. No. 17/2026. Ordinary South African passports require a visa. Up to 90 days.

Holders of a diplomatic passport issued by Türkiye are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Schedule 2 of S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026 (in force 16 February 2026). Up to 90 days.

Holders of official, service, or special passports issued by the listed states (Schedule 2, S.I. No. 473/2014 as substituted by S.I. No. 17/2026) are exempt from Irish visa requirement ONLY when arriving in the State in the company of a Minister of the Government of that state where that Minister is on an official visit to Ireland (Article 3(f) of S.I. 473/2014 as amended by S.I. 58/2024). Does NOT apply to general official/service passport holders travelling independently. Note: Georgia has been removed from Schedule 2 by S.I. 242/2026 (effective 15 June 2026) and is no longer in this list.

Holders of a service passport or public affairs passport issued by the People's Republic of China are exempt from Irish visa requirement under Article 3(f) of S.I. No. 473/2014 (as amended) ONLY when arriving in the company of a Minister of the Government of the PRC where that Minister is on an official visit to Ireland. The PRC 'service passport' and 'public affairs passport' are specifically defined in Article 2 of S.I. 473/2014.

cbi.notes: Ireland's Immigrant Investor Programme (IIP) was closed to new applications from 15 February 2023 by Minister Simon Harris. The IIP was a residency-by-investment (not citizenship-by-investment) programme. It offered four options: Enterprise Investment (min €1,000,000 for 3+ years), Investment Fund (min €1,000,000 in Central Bank-approved fund for 3+ years), Real Estate Investment Trusts (min €2,000,000 in Irish REITs for 3+ years), and Endowment (min €500,000 philanthropic donation to arts/sports/health/culture/education). No replacement CBI or RBI programme has been announced. Closure was driven by EU Commission, Council of Europe, and OECD concerns about border security, money laundering, and tax evasion. Approximately 1,500 pending applications at closure are being processed.

programs.name: Immigrant Investor Programme (IIP) - CLOSED
programs.notes: Closed to new applications from 15 February 2023. Previously offered four investment options (enterprise, fund, REIT, endowment) with minimum €500,000–€2,000,000. No active programme currently.

programs.name: Critical Skills Employment Permit
programs.eligibility: Non-EEA nationals in occupations on the Critical Skills Occupations List (ICT, engineering, technology, etc.); minimum salary €40,904/year (some roles require €68,911+); minimum 2-year job offer; relevant degree or higher; employer must be registered with Irish Revenue Commissioners.
programs.processing_time: Applications must be submitted at least 12 weeks before employment start date; processed strictly in date order. Current processing dates published at enterprise.gov.ie.
programs.notes: No Labour Market Needs Test required. Permit holders can apply for permission to reside and work without an employment permit after completion. Family reunification available. After 5 years continuous residency, eligible for long-term residence. Pathway to citizenship (naturalisation) opens after 5 years residence. Employment Permits Act 2024 governs current rules.

programs.name: Start-up Entrepreneur Programme (STEP)
programs.eligibility: Non-EEA nationals with an innovative business proposal (not retail/catering/personal services); minimum €50,000 funding from personal resources, loan, business angel, venture capital, or Irish state agency grant; additional €30,000 per additional co-founder. Business must have potential to create 10 jobs and €1 million in sales within 3–4 years.
programs.processing_time: Applications considered quarterly by an evaluation committee.
programs.notes: Successful applicants receive 2-year residence permission to reside and work in Ireland full-time in the business. Visa-required nationals receive multiple-entry visa. Programme established in 2012 and continues operating. Guidelines updated September 2023.

programs.name: General Employment Permit
programs.eligibility: Non-EEA nationals in occupations not on the exclusions list; subject to Labour Market Needs Test unless exempt.
programs.processing_time: Processed in date order; current processing dates at enterprise.gov.ie.
programs.notes: One of nine permit types under Employment Permits Act 2024. Other types include Intra-Company Transfer, Dependant/Partner/Spouse, Contract for Services, Reactivation, Internship, Sport and Cultural, and Exchange Agreement permits.

validation_notes: VERIFIED (2026-06-29): Full re-verification pass against official sources completed. irishstatutebook.ie SI index confirmed through S.I. 295/2026 — no new Immigration Act (Visas) Order statutory instruments enacted after S.I. 242/2026. WebSearch and DFA assets.ireland.ie confirm the May 2025 PDF remains the latest official nationality list. Wikipedia cross-check of visa-free nationalities consistent with this record. All visa_free entries, conditional_access entries, and CBI/RBI/fast_track sections confirmed accurate as of 2026-06-29. No changes required to nationality access classifications.

CORRECTION (2026-06-27): Saint Lucia (LCA) entry fully removed from visa_free array. As of 2026-06-27 the removal effective date (2026-06-15) has passed and the transitional window (bookings before 2026-06-15, travel before 2026-07-14) is also expiring; Saint Lucia is now unambiguously visa-required. Previously retained as a flagged entry with 'removed_effective'; now deleted entirely from visa_free to avoid any risk of a false visa-free classification. Source: S.I. No. 242/2026 (irishstatutebook.ie), confirmed by Minister Brophy press release (gov.ie).

VERIFIED (2026-06-27): irishstatutebook.ie confirms S.I. 17/2026 and S.I. 242/2026 are the only two Immigration Act (Visas) statutory instruments enacted in 2026. No further nationality changes between 15 June 2026 and 27 June 2026. All other visa_free entries remain consistent with the May 2025 official PDF and subsequent SIs.

VERIFIED (2026-06-27): No new visa-free nationality PDF has been published since May 2025. The official May 2025 PDF remains the base document; all post-May 2025 changes (S.I. 68/2025, 164/2025, 17/2026, 242/2026) are captured via statutory instrument references in this record.
NOTED (2026-06-27): As of 1 June 2026, applicants can no longer appeal the refusal of a short-stay visa. This is a procedural change only and does not affect nationality access classifications.

CORRECTION (2026-06-14): Nicaragua (NIC), Saint Kitts and Nevis (KNA), and Saint Lucia (LCA) removed from visa_free list, effective 15 June 2026 per S.I. No. 242/2026. All passport types (ordinary, diplomatic, service) of these three nationalities now require a visa. Nicaragua and Saint Kitts and Nevis entries deleted entirely; Saint Lucia entry marked with removed_effective flag and explanatory note. Source: gov.ie press release by Minister Brophy and S.I. 242/2026.

CORRECTION (2026-06-14): Georgia removed from diplomatic passport conditional_access entry (basis='passport_type', passport_types=['diplomatic']) per S.I. No. 242/2026 (effective 15 June 2026). Georgia is also removed from the service/official/special passport group entry. The prior record included Georgia in Schedule 2 (added by S.I. 17/2026); it is now removed.

CORRECTION (2026-06-14): Added note about temporary suspension of European Agreement on Abolition of Visas for Refugees (effective 19 July for 12 months). Holders of 1951 travel documents now require an Irish entry visa. Sourced from irishimmigration.ie visa-non-visa-required-nationalities page banner.

CORRECTION (2026-06-14): Clarified SSVWP country list - confirmed 22 countries (not 21 or 24) per live irishimmigration.ie SSVWP page. Ecuador was never on the current official SSVWP list (removed reference). Belarus and Russia correctly noted as removed. Ukraine noted as also unconditionally visa-free. S.I. 242/2026 source URL added to visa_policy.source_urls.

VERIFIED: S.I. 17/2026 (effective 16 February 2026) confirmed via irishstatutebook.ie - substituted Schedule 2 adding 16 countries to diplomatic passport exemption including Albania, Montenegro, Bosnia and Herzegovina, North Macedonia, Colombia, PRC, Georgia (now removed by S.I. 242/2026), Peru, Indonesia, Qatar, Kosovo, Serbia, Kuwait, South Africa, Moldova, Türkiye.
VERIFIED: CBI/RBI section unchanged - IIP closed 15 February 2023, no replacement programme announced.
VERIFIED: Fast-track work permit programmes (CSEP, STEP, General EP) confirmed still active under Employment Permits Act 2024.

ADDED (2026-06-20): visa_types array populated with all confirmed Ireland visa categories sourced from irishimmigration.ie sitemap and enterprise.gov.ie official pages. Includes 9 employment permit types, short-stay C visa sub-categories (tourist, business, medical, study, marriage, family/friend, conference, training, performance/tournament, join ship, exam), long-stay D visa types (study, employment, join family), Working Holiday Authorisation, Transit Visa, and Start-up Entrepreneur Programme. Fees confirmed from enterprise.gov.ie fees page; short-stay visa fees (~€60 single, ~€100 multiple) are standard official amounts. Note: irishimmigration.ie pages returned truncated content during this research session; visa category names confirmed from page-sitemap.xml.
gaps: OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholders: No official Irish government source (irishimmigration.ie, dfa.ie, gov.ie) was found confirming or denying a specific conditional entry exemption for OCI card holders travelling to Ireland. OCI cards are issued by India and confer Indian nationality-linked benefits, but Ireland does not appear to grant a standalone conditional entry right based on OCI status. Absent an official source, no OCI entry has been recorded.
Max stay days for most non-visa required nationals is up to 90 days at border officer discretion; not a fixed statutory entitlement stated verbatim on official page for all nationalities.
Current CSEP processing time in weeks not confirmed from official page (changes regularly); official page directs to current-processing-dates portal.
EU member state nationals were listed as a group entry rather than each enumerated individually, as the official PDF lists them individually but they are standard EU freedom-of-movement cases.

Convention travel documents: Article 3(b)(i) of S.I. 473/2014 grants visa-free access to holders of valid Convention travel documents (Geneva Convention / stateless persons documents) issued by certain EEA states for visits up to 3 months. This is a further conditional access basis not captured as a full credential entry due to its narrow application to stateless persons.

European Agreement on Abolition of Visas for Refugees suspension: exact end date and whether it will be renewed after 12 months is not yet confirmed from official source. The announcement states 'for a period of 12 months' from 19 July (year not explicitly confirmed in scraped text).

S.I. 242/2026 full text confirms Schedule 5 additions for Nicaragua, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia - all three are now visa required (added to Schedule 5). The specific processing pathway details for Schedule 5 nationalities are not further detailed in this record.
No third-country held US, Canadian, Australian, Japanese, Schengen, or GCC visa waiver found for Ireland - Ireland explicitly does not offer conditional entry based on holding these credentials.
Short-stay C visa processing times vary significantly by embassy/consulate location and peak periods - the 8–60 day range shown is an approximation; some high-volume posts may take longer.

Working Holiday Authorisation: exact eligible countries, age limits, and fees could not be confirmed from the irishimmigration.ie page due to content truncation during research. The page URL was confirmed from the sitemap.
Long-stay D visa fee: standard fee is approximately €100 but may differ by location; USD conversion approximate.
Transit visa: specific nationalities that require an Irish transit visa vs those that can transit airside without a visa were not fully extracted due to page truncation.

sources_checked.description: Official DFA/ISD list of visa-required and non-visa-required nationalities, last updated 12 May 2025
ISD visa and non-visa required nationalities page (live lookup table). Includes notice re: suspension of European Agreement on Abolition of Visas for Refugees from 19 July.
Official government press release announcing closure of the IIP from 15 February 2023
ISD FAQ on IIP closure
DETE Critical Skills Employment Permit page - confirmed fee €1,000, min 2-year validity, salary thresholds
DETE all employment permit types page - confirmed 9 permit types under Employment Permits Act 2024
DETE General Employment Permit page - confirmed fees €500-€1,000 new, €750-€1,500 renewal; min salary €36,605
DETE Intra-Company Transfer Employment Permit page - confirmed fees, salary thresholds, max 5-year stay
DETE Dependant/Partner/Spouse Employment Permit page - confirmed no fee, must already reside in Ireland
DETE Internship Employment Permit page - confirmed fees €500-€1,000, max 12 months, must be enrolled student
DETE Sport and Cultural Employment Permit page - confirmed fees, initial 2-year duration
DETE Employment Permit Fees page - confirmed full fee schedule for all 9 permit types
ISD sitemap - used to identify all visa category URLs including short-stay C sub-types, long-stay D visa types, transit, working holidays, and join family visa
ISD Start-up Entrepreneur Programme (STEP) page
ISD Short Stay Visa Waiver Programme (SSVWP) page, last updated 9 May 2025. Confirmed 22 eligible countries.
ISD British Irish Visa Scheme (BIVS) page, last updated 27 June 2024
Official gov.ie page on the Common Travel Area
S.I. No. 473/2014 - Immigration Act 2004 (Visas) Order 2014 (base order)

S.I. No. 58/2024 - Amendment Order 2024: expanded Schedule 2 (diplomatic passports) to Colombia, Georgia, Indonesia, Kuwait, Moldova, Montenegro, Peru, Qatar, Türkiye; broadened Article 3(f) to cover official/service/special passports accompanying minister
S.I. No. 335/2024 - Amendment (No. 2) Order 2024: added South Africa to Schedule 2 (diplomatic passports); removed Botswana and South Africa from Schedule 1 (visa-free ordinary passports)
S.I. No. 68/2025 - Amendment Order 2025 (in force 10 March 2025): removed Eswatini, Lesotho, Nauru from Schedule 1
S.I. No. 164/2025 - Amendment (No. 2) Order 2025 (in force 12 May 2025): removed Trinidad and Tobago from Schedule 1

S.I. No. 17/2026 - Amendment Order 2026 (in force 16 February 2026): substituted Schedule 2 to add Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Kosovo, Republic of North Macedonia, Serbia (and confirmed existing: Colombia, PRC, Georgia, Peru, Indonesia, Qatar, Kuwait, South Africa, Moldova, Türkiye); current authoritative Schedule 2 list (before S.I. 242/2026 removes Georgia)

S.I. No. 242/2026 - Amendment (No. 2) Order 2026 (in force 15 June 2026): removed Nicaragua, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia from Schedule 1 (visa-free); removed Georgia from Schedule 2 (diplomatic passport exemption); added those three nationalities to Schedule 5 (visa required). Re-confirmed 2026-06-27 as last enacted visa SI of 2026.

Irish Statute Book SI index for 2026 - verified 2026-06-29 that S.I. 17/2026 and S.I. 242/2026 are the only two Immigration Act (Visas) statutory instruments enacted in 2026. Index confirmed through S.I. 295/2026; no further visa SIs exist.

Gov.ie press release by Minister Brophy announcing new visa requirements for Nicaragua, Saint Kitts and Nevis, and Saint Lucia effective 15 June 2026. Transitional arrangements for bookings made before 15 June 2026 travelling before 14 July 2026.
